parts.rangeLabel: Read the text and answer questions 1–13

passage.text: Crop-growing skyscrapers
Crop-growing skyscrapers

By the year 2050, nearly 80% of the Earth’s population will live in urban centres. Applying the most conservative estimates to current demographic trends, the human population will increase by about three billion people by then.An estimated 109 hectares of new land (about 20% larger than Brazil) will be needed to grow enough food to feed them, if traditional farming methods continue as they are practised today. At present, throughout the world, over 80% of the land that is suitable for raising crops is in use. Historically, some 15% of that has been laid waste by poor management practices.What can be done to ensure enough food for the world’s population to live on?

The concept of indoor farming is not new, since hothouse production of tomatoes and other produce has been in vogue for some time.What is new is the urgent need to scale up this technology to accommodate another three billion people. Many believe an entirely new approach to indoor farming is required, employing cutting-edge technologies. One such proposal is for the ‘Vertical Farm’. The concept is of multi-storey buildings in which food crops are grown in environmentally controlled conditions. Situated in the heart of urban centres, they would drastically reduce the amount of transportation required to bring food to consumers.Vertical farms would need to be efficient, cheap to construct and safe to operate. If successfully implemented, proponents claim, vertical farms offer the promise of urban renewal, sustainable production of a safe and varied food supply (through year-round production of all crops), and the eventual repair of ecosystems that have been sacrificed for horizontal farming.

It took humans 10,000 years to learn how to grow most of the crops we now take for granted. Along the way, we despoiled most of the land we worked, often turning verdant, natural eco-zones into semi-arid deserts. Within that same time frame, we evolved into an urban species, in which 60% of the human population now lives vertically in cities. This means that, for the majority, we humans have shelter from the elements, yet we subject our food-bearing plants to the rigours of the great outdoors and can do no more than hope for a good weather year. However, more often than not now, due to a rapidly changing climate, that is not what happens. Massive floods, long droughts, hurricanes and severe monsoons take their toll each year, destroying millions of tons of valuable crops.

The supporters of vertical farming claim many potential advantages for the system. For instance, crops would be produced all year round,as they would be kept in artificially controlled, optimum growing conditions. There would be no weather-related crop failures due to droughts, floods or pests. All the food could be grown organically, eliminating the need for herbicides, pesticides and fertilisers.The system would greatly reduce the incidence of many infectious diseases that are acquired at the agricultural interface.Although the system would consume energy, it would return energy to the grid via methane generation from composting non-edible parts of plants.It would also dramatically reduce fossil fuel use, by cutting out the need for tractors, ploughs and shipping.

A major drawback of vertical farming, however, is that the plants would require artificial light.Without it, those plants nearest the windows would be exposed to more sunlight and grow more quickly, reducing the efficiency of the system. Single-storey greenhouses have the benefit of natural overhead light; even so, many still need artificial lighting.

A multi-story facility with no natural overhead light would require far more. Generating enough light could be prohibitively expensive, unless cheap, renewable energy is available, and this appears to be rather a future aspiration than a likelihood for the near future.

One variation on vertical farming that has been developed is to grow plants in stacked trays that move on rails. Moving the trays allows the plants to get enough sunlight. This system is already in operation, and works well within a single-story greenhouse with light reaching it from above: it is not certain, however, that it can be made to work without that overhead natural light.

Vertical farming is an attempt to address the undoubted problems that we face in producing enough food for a growing population. At the moment, though, more needs to be done to reduce the detrimental impact it would have on the environment, particularly as regards the use of energy. While it is possible that much of our food will be grown in skyscrapers in future, most experts currently believe it is far more likely that we will simply use the space available on urban rooftops.

Crop-growing skyscrapers

questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 1–7

Complete the sentences below.

Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.instruction: Questions 1–7Complete the sentences below.Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.blockHtml:
• Some food plants, including 1_______ are already grown indoors.

• Vertical farms would be located in 2_______, meaning that there would be less need to take them long distances to customers.

• Vertical farms could use methane from plants and animals to produce 3_______

• The consumption of 4_______ would be cut because agricultural vehicles would be unnecessary.

• The fact that vertical farms would need 5_______ light is a disadvantage.

• One form of vertical farming involves planting in 6_______ which are not fixed.

• The most probable development is that food will be grown on 7_______ in towns and cities.
questionGroups.noteContent:
• Some food plants, including [1]  are already grown indoors.
• Vertical farms would be located in [2], meaning that there would be less need to take them long distances to customers.
• Vertical farms could use methane from plants and animals to produce [3]
• The consumption of [4] would be cut because agricultural vehicles would be unnecessary.
• The fact that vertical farms would need [5] light is a disadvantage.
• One form of vertical farming involves planting in [6] which are not fixed.
• The most probable development is that food will be grown on [7] in towns and cities.

questions.prompt: Some food plants, including ___________ are already grown indoors.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“already grown indoors / example plants”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph 2: “hothouse production of tomatoes and other produce…”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“tomatoes” là ví dụ cụ thể của cây trồng trong nhà → điền đúng 1 từ
=> Đáp án: tomatoes
questions.questionHtml:
• Some food plants, including 1_______ are already grown indoors.

• Vertical farms would be located in 2_______, meaning that there would be less need to take them long distances to customers.

• Vertical farms could use methane from plants and animals to produce 3_______

• The consumption of 4_______ would be cut because agricultural vehicles would be unnecessary.

• The fact that vertical farms would need 5_______ light is a disadvantage.

• One form of vertical farming involves planting in 6_______ which are not fixed.

• The most probable development is that food will be grown on 7_______ in towns and cities.

questions.prompt: Vertical farms would be located in ___________, meaning that there would be less need to take them long distances to customers.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“located / where built / city”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph 2: “Situated in the heart of urban centres”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“heart of urban centres” = trung tâm đô thị
=> Đáp án: urban centres

questions.prompt: Vertical farms could use methane from plants and animals to produce ___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“methane / produce energy”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph 4: “return energy to the grid via methane generation”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
Methane → tạo ra energy
=> Đáp án: energy

questions.prompt: The consumption of ___________ would be cut because agricultural vehicles would be unnecessary.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“cut / vehicles / fuel”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph 4: “reduce fossil fuel use, by cutting out tractors, ploughs and shipping”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
loại nhiên liệu dùng cho phương tiện nông nghiệp = fossil fuel
=> Đáp án: fossil fuel

questions.prompt: The fact that vertical farms would need ___________ light is a disadvantage.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “need / artificial / light”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 5: “A major drawback of vertical farming, however, is that the plants would require artificial light.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: 
Trở ngại (drawback) của nông trại dọc là cây trồng yêu cầu (require) ánh sáng nhân tạo.
=> Đáp án: artificial

questions.prompt: One form of vertical farming involves planting in ___________ which are not fixed.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“planting”
“stacked trays”
“not fixed”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 6: “One variation on vertical farming that has been developed is to grow plants in stacked trays that move on rails.”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
Di chuyển trên đường ray (move on rails) nghĩa là không cố định. Cây được trồng trong các khay xếp chồng.
=> Đáp án: stacked trays

questions.prompt: The most probable development is that food will be grown on ___________ in towns and cities.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “probable development”
“urban rooftops”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 7: “...most experts currently believe it is far more likely that we will simply use the space available on urban rooftops.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: 
Xu hướng có khả năng xảy ra cao nhất (far more likely) là tận dụng không gian trên sân thượng đô thị.
=> Đáp án: urban rooftops

questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 8–13

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 8–13 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questionGroups.instruction: Questions 8–13Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?In boxes 8–13 on your answer sheet, writeTRUE if the statement agrees with the informationFALSE if the statement contradicts the informationNOT GIVEN if there is no information on this
questionGroups.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.prompt: Methods for predicting the Earth's population have recently changed.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“methods”
“predicting”
“population”
“changed”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 1: “Applying the most conservative estimates to current demographic trends, the human population will increase...”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ đưa ra số liệu dự báo dựa trên xu hướng hiện tại, hoàn toàn không nói đến việc phương pháp dự báo có bị thay đổi hay không.
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Human beings are responsible for some of the destruction to food-producing land.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“human”
“responsible”
“destruction”
“land”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 3: “Along the way, we despoiled most of the land we worked, often turning verdant, natural eco-zones into semi-arid deserts.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: "We" (con người) làm tổn hại (despoiled) phần lớn đất đai canh tác biến chúng thành hoang mạc. Con người chính là tác nhân gây hủy hoại.
=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: The crops produced in vertical farms will depend on the season.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“vertical farms”
“depend”
“season”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Paragraph 4: “...crops would be produced all year round, as they would be kept in artificially controlled, optimum growing conditions.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Cây trồng được sản xuất quanh năm (all year round) nhờ môi trường kiểm soát nhân tạo, tức là không phụ thuộc vào mùa màng. Thông tin trái ngược với câu hỏi.
=> Đáp án: FALSE

questions.prompt: Some damage to food crops is caused by climate change.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“damage”
“crops”
“climate change”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 3: “However, more often than not now, due to a rapidly changing climate... destroying millions of tons of valuable crops.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Do khí hậu thay đổi nhanh chóng (due to a rapidly changing climate) dẫn đến thiên tai hủy hoại hàng triệu tấn nông sản.
=>  Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: Fertilisers will be needed for certain crops in vertical farms.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“fertilisers”
“needed”
“vertical farms”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Paragraph 4: “All the food could be grown organically, eliminating the need for herbicides, pesticides and fertilisers.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Canh tác hữu cơ giúp loại bỏ hoàn toàn nhu cầu (eliminating the need) sử dụng phân bón hóa học. Thông tin trái ngược với câu hỏi.
Đáp án: FALSE

questions.prompt: Vertical farming will make plants less likely to be affected by infectious diseases.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“less likely”
“affected”
“infectious diseases”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 4: “The system would greatly reduce the incidence of many infectious diseases that are acquired at the agricultural interface.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: 
Hệ thống này giảm mạnh tỷ lệ mắc (reduce the incidence) các bệnh truyền nhiễm, nghĩa là cây ít có nguy cơ bị nhiễm bệnh hơn.
Đáp án: TRUE

parts.rangeLabel: Read the text and answer questions 14–26

passage.text: The Falkirk Wheel
A unique engineering achievement

The Falkirk Wheel in Scotland is the world’s first and only rotating boat lift. Opened in 2002, it is central to the ambitious £84.5m Millennium Link project to restore navigability across Scotland by reconnecting the historic waterways of the Forth & Clyde and Union Canals.

The major challenge of the project lays in the fact that the Forth & Clyde Canal is situated 35 metres below the level of the Union Canal. Historically, the two canals had been joined near the town of Falkirk by a sequence of 11 locks - enclosed sections of canal in which the water level could be raised or lowered - that stepped down across a distance of 1.5 km. This had been dismantled in 1933, thereby breaking the link.When the project was launched in 1994, the British Waterways authority was keen to create a dramatic twenty-first-century landmark which would not only be a fitting commemoration of the Millennium, but also a lasting symbol of the economic regeneration of the region.

Numerous ideas were submitted for the project, including concepts ranging from rolling eggs to tilting tanks, from giant seesaws to overhead monorails. The eventual winner was a plan for the huge rotating steel boat lift which was to become The Falkirk Wheel. The unique shape of the structure is claimed to have been inspired by various sources, both manmade and natural, most notably a Celtic double-headed axe, but also the vast turning propeller of a ship, the ribcage of a whale or the spine of a fish.

The various parts of The Falkirk Wheel were all constructed and assembled, like one giant toy building set, at Butterley Engineering’s Steelworks in Derbyshire, some 400 km from Falkirk.A team there carefully assembled the 1,200 tonnes of steel, painstakingly fitting the pieces together to an accuracy of just 10 mm to ensure a perfect final fit. In the summer of 2001, the structure was then dismantled and transported on 35 lorries to Falkirk, before all being bolted back together again on the ground, and finally lifted into position in five large sections by crane. The Wheel would need to withstand immense and constantly changing stresses as it rotated, so to make the structure more robust, the steel sections were bolted rather than welded together. Over 45,000 bolt holes were matched with their bolts, and each bolt was hand-tightened.

The Wheel consists of two sets of opposing axe-shaped arms, attached about 25 metres apart to a fixed central spine. Two diametrically opposed water-filled ’gondolas’, each with a capacity of 360,000 litres, are fitted between the ends of the arms. These gondolas always weigh the same, whether or not they are carrying boats. This is because, according to Archimedes’ principle of displacement, floating objects displace their own weight in water. So when a boat enters a gondola, the amount of water leaving the gondola weighs exactly the same as the boat. This keeps the Wheel balanced and so, despite its enormous mass, it rotates through 180° in five and a half minutes while using very little power. It takes just 1.5 kilowatt-hours (5.4 MJ) of energy to rotate the Wheel - roughly the same as boiling eight small domestic kettles of water.

Boats needing to be lifted up enter the canal basin at the level of the Forth & Clyde Canal and then enter the lower gondola of the Wheel. Two hydraulic steel gates are raised, so as to seal the gondola off from the water in the canal basin. The water between the gates is then pumped out. A hydraulic clamp, which prevents the arms of the Wheel moving while the gondola is docked, is removed, allowing the Wheel to turn.In the central machine room an array of ten hydraulic motors then begins to rotate the central axle.The axle connects to the outer arms of the Wheel, which begin to rotate at a speed of 1/8 of a revolution per minute. As the wheel rotates, the gondolas are kept in the upright position by a simple gearing system. Two eight-metre-wide cogs orbit a fixed inner cog of the same width, connected by two smaller cogs travelling in the opposite direction to the outer cogs - so ensuring that the gondolas always remain level.When the gondola reaches the top, the boat passes straight onto the aqueduct situated 24 metres above the canal basin.

The remaining 11 metres of lift needed to reach the Union Canal is achieved by means of a pair of locks. The Wheel could not be constructed to elevate boats over the full 35-metre difference between the two canals, owing to the presence of the historically important Antonine Wall, which was built by the Romans in the second century AD. Boats travel under this wall via a tunnel, then through the locks, and finally on to the Union Canal.

questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 14–19

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 14–19 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questionGroups.instruction: Questions 14–19Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?In boxes 14–19 on your answer sheet, writeTRUE if the statement agrees with the informationFALSE if the statement contradicts the informationNOT GIVEN if there is no information on this
questionGroups.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.prompt: The Falkirk Wheel has linked the Forth & Clyde Canal with the Union Canal for the first time in their history.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“Falkirk Wheel”
“linked”
“first time”
“history”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph 2: “Historically, the two canals had been joined near the town of Falkirk by a sequence of 11 locks... This had been dismantled in 1933, thereby breaking the link.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: 
Trong lịch sử hai con kênh này đã từng được nối với nhau (had been joined) bằng một chuỗi 11 âu thuyền trước khi bị tháo dỡ vào năm 1933. Do đó, việc Falkirk Wheel kết nối chúng không phải là lần đầu tiên trong lịch sử. Thông tin trái ngược với câu hỏi.
=> Đáp án: FALSE

questions.prompt: There was some opposition to the design of the Falkirk Wheel at first.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“opposition”
“design”
“at first”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 3: “Numerous ideas were submitted for the project, including concepts ranging from rolling eggs to tilting tanks... The eventual winner was a plan for the huge rotating steel boat lift...”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn chỉ nói có nhiều ý tưởng thiết kế khác nhau được đệ trình và phương án cuối cùng được chọn, hoàn toàn không có thông tin nói về việc có sự phản đối hay phản kháng nào đối với thiết kế này lúc ban đầu hay không.
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: The Falkirk Wheel was initially put together at the location where its components were manufactured.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“initially”
“put together”
“location”
“manufactured”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 4: “The various parts of The Falkirk Wheel were all constructed and assembled... at Butterley Engineering's Steelworks in Derbyshire, some 400 km from Falkirk.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Các bộ phận được sản xuất (constructed) và lắp ráp thử ban đầu (assembled = put together) ngay tại nhà máy thép ở Derbyshire - nơi chúng được chế tạo, trước khi rã ra mang đến Falkirk. Thông tin hoàn toàn trùng khớp.
=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: The Falkirk Wheel is the only boat lift in the world which has steel sections bolted together by hand.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“only boat lift”
“world”
“bolted together by hand”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 4: “Over 45,000 bolt holes were matched with their bolts, and each bolt was hand-tightened.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc có đề cập việc các bu-lông được siết bằng tay (hand-tightened), tuy nhiên không hề có thông tin khẳng định đây là thang nâng thuyền "duy nhất trên thế giới" (only... in the world) có các phần thép được bắt bu-lông bằng tay.
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: The weight of the gondolas varies according to the size of boat being carried.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“weight”
“gondolas”
“varies”
“size of boat”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph 5: “These gondolas always weigh the same, whether or not they are carrying boats. This is because, according to Archimedes' principle of displacement, floating objects displace their own weight in water.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: 
Trọng lượng các máng nước này luôn giữ nguyên (always weigh the same), dù có chở tàu hay không do nguyên lý của Ác-si-mét. Do đó, trọng lượng không hề thay đổi (varies) theo kích thước tàu. Thông tin trái ngược với câu hỏi.
=> Đáp án: FALSE

questions.prompt: The construction of the Falkirk Wheel site took into account the presence of a nearby ancient monument.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“construction”
“ancient monument”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 7: “The Wheel could not be constructed to elevate boats over the full 35-metre difference... owing to the presence of the historically important Antonine Wall, which was built by the Romans in the second century AD.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Việc xây dựng không thể nâng độ cao toàn bộ 35m vì sự hiện diện của bức tường cổ Antonine Wall do người La Mã xây từ thế kỷ thứ 2 (ancient monument). Điều này chứng tỏ họ phải xem xét, tính toán đến di tích này khi xây dựng.
=> Đáp án: TRUE

questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 20–26

Complete the notes below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.instruction: Questions 20–26Complete the notes below.Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.blockHtml:
• A pair of 20_______ are lifted in order to shut out water from canal basin.
  • A 21_______ is taken out, enabling Wheel to rotate.

Hydraulic motors drive 22_______.

  • A range of different-sized 23_______ ensures boat keeps upright
  • Boat reaches top Wheel, then moves directly onto 24_______.
  • Boat travels through tunnel beneath Roman 25_______.
  • 26_______ raise boat 11m to level of Union Canal.
questionGroups.noteContent:
• A pair of [20] are lifted in order to shut out water from canal basin.
• A [21]  is taken out, enabling Wheel to rotate.
Hydraulic motors drive [22].
• A range of different-sized [23] ensures boat keeps upright 
• Boat reaches top Wheel, then moves directly onto  [24].
• Boat travels through tunnel beneath Roman [25].
• [26] raise boat 11m to level of Union Canal.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “A pair of”, “gates”, “lifted”, “shut out water”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 6: “Two hydraulic steel gates are raised, so as to seal the gondola off from the water in the canal basin.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “A pair of” tương đương với “Two”, động từ “lifted” tương đương với “raised”, và cụm “shut out water từ canal basin” đồng nghĩa với “seal the gondola off from the water in the canal basin”. Từ đứng giữa số lượng và danh từ gates là cụm từ bổ nghĩa chỉ chất liệu/loại cổng: “hydraulic steel”. Đề bài yêu cầu điền MỘT TỪ (Choose ONE WORD), ta chọn tính từ cốt lõi mang nghĩa chức năng là gates (hoặc thép, nhưng trong ngữ cảnh này từ khớp kỹ thuật chuẩn cho vị trí là từ chỉ bộ phận: gates đã có sẵn, vậy từ cần điền bổ nghĩa trước nó chính là loại cổng). Nhìn lại đề bài đã cho sẵn từ gates, nên từ điền vào vị trí trống là thành phần đứng trước nó. Theo đáp án chuẩn từ gốc bài thi là gates (ở đây đề của bạn bị lặp từ gates ở ngoài, từ cần điền đúng cấu trúc gốc của sơ đồ là loại cổng).

=> Đáp án: gates
questions.questionHtml:
• A pair of 20_______ are lifted in order to shut out water from canal basin.
  • A 21_______ is taken out, enabling Wheel to rotate.

Hydraulic motors drive 22_______.

  • A range of different-sized 23_______ ensures boat keeps upright
  • Boat reaches top Wheel, then moves directly onto 24_______.
  • Boat travels through tunnel beneath Roman 25_______.
  • 26_______ raise boat 11m to level of Union Canal.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“taken out”, “enabling Wheel to rotate” (cho phép bánh xe quay)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 6: “A hydraulic clamp, which prevents the arms of the Wheel moving while the gondola is docked, is removed, allowing the Wheel to turn.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “taken out” đồng nghĩa với “is removed”, cụm “enabling... to rotate” đồng nghĩa với “allowing... to turn”. Vật thể được lấy ra để bánh xe bắt đầu quay là clamp (cái kẹp).

=> Đáp án: clamp

Bước 1: Keyword: *“Hydraulic motors”, “drive” (vận hành/làm quay)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 6: “In the central machine room, an array of ten hydraulic motors then begins to rotate the central axle.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “drive” trong sơ đồ kỹ thuật đồng nghĩa với hành động làm cho quay (rotate). Đối tượng chịu tác động trực tiếp từ các motor thủy lực này là axle (trục trung tâm).

=> Đáp án: axle

Bước 1: Keyword: *“range of different-sized”, “ensures boat keeps upright” (đảm bảo thuyền luôn thẳng đứng)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 6: “As the wheel rotates, the gondolas are kept in the upright position by a simple gearing system. Two eight-metre-wide cogs orbit a fixed inner cog of the same width, connected by two smaller cogs...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Để giữ cho các gondola chứa thuyền luôn thẳng đứng (upright / level), hệ thống sử dụng các bánh răng có kích thước khác nhau (gồm bánh răng rộng 8m và các bánh răng nhỏ hơn – smaller cogs). Danh từ số nhiều chỉ các bánh răng này là cogs.

=> Đáp án: cogs

Bước 1: Keyword: “reaches top Wheel”, “moves directly onto” (di chuyển thẳng vào)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 6: “When the gondola reaches the top, the boat passes straight onto the aqueduct situated 24 metres above the canal basin.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “moves directly onto” đồng nghĩa với “passes straight onto”. Nơi mà con thuyền sẽ đi thẳng vào khi lên tới đỉnh chính là aqueduct (máng dẫn nước/cầu kênh).

=> Đáp án: aqueduct

Bước 1: Keyword: “travel under”, “via a tunnel” (qua một đường hầm)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 7: “Boats travel under this wall via a tunnel, then through the locks, and finally on to the Union Canal.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Câu hỏi yêu cầu tìm danh từ chỉ vật thể mà thuyền phải đi phía dưới nó thông qua một đường hầm. Đối chiếu từ “under this...” với đoạn văn, từ đứng sau chính là wall (bức tường Antonine).

=> Đáp án: wall

Bước 1: Keyword: “then through the”, “finally on to the Union Canal”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: ĐOẠN 7: “Boats travel under this wall via a tunnel, then through the locks, and finally on to the Union Canal.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đây là bước cuối cùng trong chuỗi hành trình của con thuyền để tới được kênh Union. Từ nằm ở giữa cụm “then through the” và “and finally” trong đoạn văn chính là danh từ số nhiều locks (các âu thuyền).

=> Đáp án: locks

parts.rangeLabel: Read the text and answer questions 27–40
parts.passageTitle: Reducing the Effects of Climate Change

passage.text: The Effects of Climate Change
Reducing the Effects of Climate Change

Such is our dependence on fossil fuels, and such is the volume of carbon dioxide already released into the atmosphere, that many experts agree that significant global warming is now inevitable. They believe that the best we can do is keep it at a reasonable level, and at present the only serious option for doing this is cutting back on our carbon emissions. But while a few countries are making major strides in this regard, the majority are having great difficulty even stemming the rate of increase, let alone reversing it. Consequently, an increasing number of scientists are beginning to explore the alternative of geo-engineering — a term which generally refers to the intentional large-scale manipulation of the environment. According to its proponents, geo-engineering is the equivalent of a backup generator: if Plan A – reducing our dependency on fossil fuels – fails, we require a Plan B, employing grand schemes to slow down or reverse the process of global warming.

Geo-engineering; has been shown to work, at least on a small localised scale. For decades, MayDay parades in Moscow have taken place under clear blue skies, aircraft having deposited dry ice, silver iodide and cement powder to disperse clouds. Many of the schemes now suggested look to do the opposite, and reduce the amount of sunlight reaching the planet. The most eye-catching idea of all is suggested by Professor Roger Angel of the University of Arizona. His scheme would employ up to 16 trillion minute spacecraft, each weighing about one gram, to form a transparent, sunlight-refracting sunshade in an orbit 1.5 million km above the Earth. This could, argues Angel, reduce the amount of light reaching the Earth by two per cent.

The majority of geo-engineering projects so far carried out — which include planting forests in deserts and depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae – have focused on achieving a general cooling of the Earth. But some look specifically at reversing the melting at the poles, particularly the Arctic. The reasoning is that if you replenish the ice sheets and frozen waters of the high latitudes, more light will be reflected back into space, so reducing the warming of the oceans and atmosphere.

The concept of releasing aerosol sprays into the stratosphere above the Arctic has been proposed by several scientists. This would involve using sulphur or hydrogen sulphide aerosols so that sulphur dioxide would form clouds, which would, in turn, lead to a global dimming. The idea is modelled on historic volcanic explosions, such as that of Mount Pinatubo in the Philippines in 1991, which led to a short-term cooling of global temperatures by 0.5 °C. Scientists have also scrutinised whether it’s possible to preserve the ice sheets of Greenland with reinforced high-tension cables, preventing icebergs from moving into the sea. Meanwhile in the Russian Arctic, geo-engineering plans include the planting of millions of birch trees. Whereas the regions’ native evergreen pines shade the snow and absorb radiation, birches would shed their leaves in winter, thus enabling radiation to be reflected by the snow. Re-routing Russian rivers to increase cold water flow to ice-forming areas could also be used to slow down warming, say some climate scientists.

But will such schemes ever be implemented? Generally speaking, those who are most cautious about geo-engineering are the scientists involved in the research. Angel says that his plan is ‘no substitute for developing renewable energy: the only permanent solution’. And Dr Phil Rasch of the US-based Pacific Northwest National Laboratory is equally guarded about the role of geo-engineering: ‘I think all of us agree that if we were to end geo-engineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within ten to twenty years. That’s certainly something to worry about.’

The US National Center for Atmospheric Research has already suggested that the proposal to inject sulphur into the atmosphere might affect rainfall patterns across the tropics and the Southern Ocean. ‘Geo-engineering plans to inject stratospheric aerosols or to seed clouds would act to cool the planet, and act to increase the extent of sea ice,’ says Rasch. ‘But all the models suggest some impact on the distribution of precipitation.’

’A further risk with geo-engineering projects is that you can “overshoot”,’ says Dr Dan Hunt, from the University of Bristol’s School of Geophysical Sciences, who has studied the likely impacts of the sunshade and aerosol schemes on the climate. ‘You may bring global temperatures back to pre-industrial levels, but the risk is that the poles will still be warmer than they should be and the tropics will be cooler than before industrialisation.’ To avoid such a scenario,” Hunt says, “Angel’s project would have to operate at half strength; all of which reinforces his view that the best option is to avoid the need for geo-engineering altogether.”

The main reason why geo-engineering is supported by many in the scientific community is that most researchers have little faith in the ability of politicians to agree – and then bring in — the necessary carbon cuts. Even leading conservation organisations see the value of investigating the potential of geo-engineering. According to Dr Martin Sommerkorn, climate change advisor for the World Wildlife Fund’s International Arctic Programme, ‘Human-induced climate change has brought humanity to a position where we shouldn’t exclude thinking thoroughly about this topic and its possibilities.’

parts.passageHtml:
The Effects of Climate Change

Reducing the Effects of Climate Change

A.

Such is our dependence on fossil fuels, and such is the volume of carbon dioxide already released into the atmosphere, that many experts agree that significant global warming is now inevitable. They believe that the best we can do is keep it at a reasonable level, and at present the only serious option for doing this is cutting back on our carbon emissions. But while a few countries are making major strides in this regard, the majority are having great difficulty even stemming the rate of increase, let alone reversing it. Consequently, an increasing number of scientists are beginning to explore the alternative of geo-engineering — a term which generally refers to the intentional large-scale manipulation of the environment. According to its proponents, geo-engineering is the equivalent of a backup generator: if Plan A – reducing our dependency on fossil fuels – fails, we require a Plan B, employing grand schemes to slow down or reverse the process of global warming.

B.

Geo-engineering; has been shown to work, at least on a small localised scale. For decades, MayDay parades in Moscow have taken place under clear blue skies, aircraft having deposited dry ice, silver iodide and cement powder to disperse clouds. Many of the schemes now suggested look to do the opposite, and reduce the amount of sunlight reaching the planet. The most eye-catching idea of all is suggested by Professor Roger Angel of the University of Arizona. His scheme would employ up to 16 trillion minute spacecraft, each weighing about one gram, to form a transparent, sunlight-refracting sunshade in an orbit 1.5 million km above the Earth. This could, argues Angel, reduce the amount of light reaching the Earth by two per cent.

C.

The majority of geo-engineering projects so far carried out — which include planting forests in deserts and depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae – have focused on achieving a general cooling of the Earth. But some look specifically at reversing the melting at the poles, particularly the Arctic. The reasoning is that if you replenish the ice sheets and frozen waters of the high latitudes, more light will be reflected back into space, so reducing the warming of the oceans and atmosphere.

D.

The concept of releasing aerosol sprays into the stratosphere above the Arctic has been proposed by several scientists. This would involve using sulphur or hydrogen sulphide aerosols so that sulphur dioxide would form clouds, which would, in turn, lead to a global dimming. The idea is modelled on historic volcanic explosions, such as that of Mount Pinatubo in the Philippines in 1991, which led to a short-term cooling of global temperatures by 0.5 °C. Scientists have also scrutinised whether it’s possible to preserve the ice sheets of Greenland with reinforced high-tension cables, preventing icebergs from moving into the sea. Meanwhile in the Russian Arctic, geo-engineering plans include the planting of millions of birch trees. Whereas the regions’ native evergreen pines shade the snow and absorb radiation, birches would shed their leaves in winter, thus enabling radiation to be reflected by the snow. Re-routing Russian rivers to increase cold water flow to ice-forming areas could also be used to slow down warming, say some climate scientists.

E.

But will such schemes ever be implemented? Generally speaking, those who are most cautious about geo-engineering are the scientists involved in the research. Angel says that his plan is ‘no substitute for developing renewable energy: the only permanent solution’. And Dr Phil Rasch of the US-based Pacific Northwest National Laboratory is equally guarded about the role of geo-engineering: ‘I think all of us agree that if we were to end geo-engineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within ten to twenty years. That’s certainly something to worry about.’

F.

The US National Center for Atmospheric Research has already suggested that the proposal to inject sulphur into the atmosphere might affect rainfall patterns across the tropics and the Southern Ocean. ‘Geo-engineering plans to inject stratospheric aerosols or to seed clouds would act to cool the planet, and act to increase the extent of sea ice,’ says Rasch. ‘But all the models suggest some impact on the distribution of precipitation.’

G.

’A further risk with geo-engineering projects is that you can “overshoot”,’ says Dr Dan Hunt, from the University of Bristol’s School of Geophysical Sciences, who has studied the likely impacts of the sunshade and aerosol schemes on the climate. ‘You may bring global temperatures back to pre-industrial levels, but the risk is that the poles will still be warmer than they should be and the tropics will be cooler than before industrialisation.’ To avoid such a scenario,” Hunt says, “Angel’s project would have to operate at half strength; all of which reinforces his view that the best option is to avoid the need for geo-engineering altogether.”

H.

The main reason why geo-engineering is supported by many in the scientific community is that most researchers have little faith in the ability of politicians to agree – and then bring in — the necessary carbon cuts. Even leading conservation organisations see the value of investigating the potential of geo-engineering. According to Dr Martin Sommerkorn, climate change advisor for the World Wildlife Fund’s International Arctic Programme, ‘Human-induced climate change has brought humanity to a position where we shouldn’t exclude thinking thoroughly about this topic and its possibilities.’

questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 27–29

Reading Passage has sections A–H. Which section contains the following information?

Write the correct letter, A–H, in boxes 27–29 on your answer sheet.
questionGroups.instruction: Questions 27–29Reading Passage has sections A–H. Which section contains the following information?Write the correct letter, A–H, in boxes 27–29 on your answer sheet.
questionGroups.blockHtml:
List of options

A. A

B. B

C. C

D. D

E. E

F. F

G. G

H. H

27_______  mention of a geo-engineering project based on an earlier natural phenomenon

28_______  an example of a successful use of geo-engineering

29_______  a common definition of geo-engineering
questionGroups.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.prompt: mention of a geo-engineering project based on an earlier natural phenomenon
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “geo-engineering project”, “based on”, “earlier natural phenomenon” (hiện tượng tự nhiên trước đó)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn D: “The idea is modelled on historic volcanic explosions, such as that of Mount Pinatubo in the Philippines in 1991, which led to a short-term cooling of global temperatures by 0.5 °C.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Ý tưởng phun khí aerosol được mô phỏng theo (modelled on) các vụ phun trào núi lửa trong lịch sử (historic volcanic explosions). Núi lửa phun trào chính là một hiện tượng tự nhiên đã xảy ra trong quá khứ.
=> Đáp án: D

questions.prompt: an example of a successful use of geo-engineering
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “an example”, “successful use” (sử dụng thành công/hiệu quả)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “Geo-engineering; has been shown to work, at least on a small localised scale. For decades, MayDay parades in Moscow have taken place under clear blue skies, aircraft having deposited dry ice, silver iodide and cement powder to disperse clouds.”  
Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “has been shown to work” đồng nghĩa với việc ứng dụng thành công. Việc các buổi diễu hành ở Moscow luôn diễn ra dưới trời xanh nhờ máy bay rải hóa chất làm tan mây chính là ví dụ thực tế cho sự thành công này.
=> Đáp án: B

questions.prompt: a common definition of geo-engineering
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “common definition” (định nghĩa phổ biến)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn A: “...an increasing number of scientists are beginning to explore the alternative of geo-engineering — a term which generally refers to the intentional large-scale manipulation of the environment.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “a term which generally refers to...” (một thuật ngữ mà nhìn chung chỉ về...) là cấu trúc dùng để đưa ra định nghĩa cho khái niệm địa kỹ thuật.
=> Đáp án: A

questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 30–36

Complete the table below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.instruction: Questions 30–36Complete the table below.Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.blockHtml:
ProcedureAim
put a large number of tiny spacecraft into orbit far above Earthto create a 30_______ that would reduce the amount of light reaching Earth
place 31_______ in the seato encourage 32_______ to form
release aerosol sprays into the stratosphereto create 33_______ that would reduce the amount of light reaching Earth
fix strong 34_______ to Greenland ice sheetsto prevent icebergs moving into the sea
plant trees in Russian Arctic that would lose their leaves in winterto allow the 35_______ to reflect radiation
change the direction of 36_______to bring more cold water into ice-forming areas
questionGroups.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.prompt: to create a 30. ________ that would reduce the amount of light reaching Earth
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Roger Angel”, “spacecraft”, “orbit”, “form a...”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “His scheme would employ up to 16 trillion minute spacecraft... to form a transparent, sunlight-refracting sunshade in an orbit 1.5 million km above the Earth.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc yêu cầu tìm danh từ chỉ vật thể được tạo ra trên quỹ đạo nhằm cản ánh sáng mặt trời. Từ đứng ngay sau cấu trúc “form a...” trong đoạn văn là cụm tính từ bổ nghĩa và danh từ chính sunshade.
=> Đáp án: sunshade
questions.questionHtml:
ProcedureAim
put a large number of tiny spacecraft into orbit far above Earthto create a 30_______ that would reduce the amount of light reaching Earth
place 31_______ in the seato encourage 32_______ to form
release aerosol sprays into the stratosphereto create 33_______ that would reduce the amount of light reaching Earth
fix strong 34_______ to Greenland ice sheetsto prevent icebergs moving into the sea
plant trees in Russian Arctic that would lose their leaves in winterto allow the 35_______ to reflect radiation
change the direction of 36_______to bring more cold water into ice-forming areas

questions.prompt: place 31. ________ in the sea
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “planting forests”, “deserts”, “depositing... in the ocean”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn C: “The majority of geo-engineering projects so far carried out — which include planting forests in deserts and depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae...”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Đề bài cần tìm một chất được thả/đặt xuống đại dương (depositing... in the ocean). Đối chiếu với cấu trúc liệt kê song song trong bài đọc, từ đi ngay sau động từ depositing là iron.
=> Đáp án: iron

questions.prompt: to encourage 32. ________ to form
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “depositing iron”, “stimulate the growth of”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn C: “...depositing iron in the ocean to stimulate the growth of algae - have focused on achieving a general cooling of the Earth.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “stimulate the growth of” xuất hiện y hệt trong bài đọc. Đối tượng được kích thích tăng trưởng ở đây là loài tảo. Do đó ta chọn danh từ đứng ngay phía sau.
=> Đáp án: algae

questions.prompt: release aerosol sprays into the stratosphere to create 33. ________ that would reduce the amount of light reaching Earth
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “aerosol sprays”, “stratosphere”, “sulphur dioxide”, “form”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn D: “This would involve using sulphur or hydrogen sulphide aerosols so that sulphur dioxide would form clouds, which would, in turn, lead to a global dimming.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Câu hỏi tìm kiếm kết quả được tạo ra sau khi hạt sulphur dioxide hình thành (form). Danh từ đóng vai trò tân ngữ đứng ngay sau động từ form trong câu là clouds.
=> Đáp án: clouds

questions.prompt: fix strong 34. ________ to Greenland ice sheets to prevent icebergs moving into the sea
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “preserve”, “ice sheets”, “Greenland”, “reinforced”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn D: “Scientists have also scrutinised whether it's possible to preserve the ice sheets of Greenland with reinforced high-tension cables...”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Đề bài cần một danh từ chỉ vật liệu được gia cố (reinforced) dùng để giữ các tảng băng. Trong bài đọc, cụm từ đi sau tính từ reinforced là cụm danh từ high-tension cables, từ cốt lõi ở đây là danh từ số nhiều cables.
=> Đáp án: cables

questions.prompt: plant trees in Russian Arctic that would lose their leaves in winter to allow the 35. ________ to reflect radiation
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “birch trees”, “shed their leaves”, “reflected by the”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn D: “...birches would shed their leaves in winter, thus enabling radiation to be reflected by the snow.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “enabling radiation to be reflected by” ở câu hỏi khớp hoàn toàn với cấu trúc bị động trong bài đọc. Tác nhân thực hiện hành động phản xạ bức xạ này là lớp tuyết.
=> Đáp án: snow

questions.prompt: change the direction of 36. ________ to bring more cold water into ice-forming areas
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Re-routing”, “Russian rivers”, “to increase...”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn D: “Re-routing Russian rivers to increase cold water flow to ice-forming areas could also be used to slow down warming...”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Câu hỏi muốn tìm đối tượng của hành động đổi hướng (re-routing / re-routed) tại khu vực nước Nga. Danh từ trung tâm đứng ngay sau động từ dạng V-ing này là rivers.
=> Đáp án: rivers

questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 37–40

Match each statement with the correct option, A–D.

Write the correct letter in boxes 37–40 on your answer sheet.
questionGroups.instruction: Questions 37–40Match each statement with the correct option, A–D.Write the correct letter in boxes 37–40 on your answer sheet.
questionGroups.blockHtml:
List of features

A  Roger Angel

B  Phil Rasch

C  Dan Hunt

D  Martin Sommerkorn

37_______ The effects of geo-engineering may not be long-lasting.

38_______ Geo-engineering is a topic worth exploring.

39_______ It may be necessary to limit the effectiveness of geo-engineering projects.

40_______ Research into non-fossil-based fuels cannot be replaced by geo-engineering.
questionGroups.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.prompt: The effects of geo-engineering may not be long-lasting.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “effects”, “may not be long-lasting” (có thể không kéo dài lâu)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn E: “And Dr Phil Rasch... 'I think all of us agree that if we were to end geo-engineering on a given day, then the planet would return to its pre-engineered condition very rapidly, and probably within ten to twenty years.' ”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Tiến sĩ Phil Rasch lập luận rằng nếu ngừng địa kỹ thuật, hành tinh sẽ quay lại trạng thái ban đầu rất nhanh (very rapidly), chứng tỏ hiệu quả của phương pháp này chỉ mang tính tạm thời, không lâu dài.
=> Đáp án: B
questions.questionHtml:
List of features

A  Roger Angel

B  Phil Rasch

C  Dan Hunt

D  Martin Sommerkorn

37_______ The effects of geo-engineering may not be long-lasting.

38_______ Geo-engineering is a topic worth exploring.

39_______ It may be necessary to limit the effectiveness of geo-engineering projects.

40_______ Research into non-fossil-based fuels cannot be replaced by geo-engineering.

questions.prompt: Geo-engineering is a topic worth exploring.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “topic”, “worth exploring” (đáng để tìm hiểu/khám phá kỹ)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn H: “According to Dr Martin Sommerkorn... ‘Human-induced climate change has brought humanity to a position where we shouldn’t exclude thinking thoroughly about this topic and its possibilities.’ ”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Quan điểm của Martin Sommerkorn là không nên loại trừ việc suy nghĩ thấu đáo (shouldn't exclude thinking thoroughly) về chủ đề này, cụm từ này đồng nghĩa hoàn toàn với cụm “worth exploring”.
=> Đáp án: D

questions.prompt: It may be necessary to limit the effectiveness of geo-engineering projects.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “necessary”, “limit the effectiveness” (giới hạn hiệu quả/công suất)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn G: “To avoid such a scenario,” Hunt (Lunt) says, “Angel’s project would have to operate at half strength...”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Chuyên gia Dan Lunt chỉ ra rằng để tránh rủi ro hệ thống hoạt động quá đà làm đảo lộn khí hậu, dự án phải chạy ở mức một nửa công suất (operate at half strength). Đây chính là hành động nhằm giới hạn mức độ hiệu quả.
=> Đáp án: C

questions.prompt: Research into non-fossil-based fuels cannot be replaced by geo-engineering.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “non-fossil-based fuels” (nhiên liệu không hóa thạch), “cannot be replaced” (không thể bị thay thế)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn E: “Angel says that his plan is ‘no substitute for developing renewable energy: the only permanent solution'. ”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Roger Angel khẳng định dự án của ông không phải là sự thay thế (no substitute – tương đương với cannot be replaced) cho việc phát triển năng lượng tái tạo (renewable energy – tương đương với cụm từ non-fossil-based fuels).
=> Đáp án: A
